def: s.m.f. Striking, smiting; attacking; wounding, &c. (see mārnā); warlike; destructive, deadly, fatal; — striker, smiter, killer, &c. (generally as last member of compounds); — s.m. A kind of deadly weapon made of two antelope-horns tipped with steel, and joined at their bases; — (dialec.) lord, husband; dear one, love; — a species of egg-plant, Solanum diffusum, and its fruit; — mārū-baiṅgon, s.m. Idem.
more: n. m. 1. Lord; husband. 2. My dear or love. 3. A class of songs. mārū baiṅgan , n. m. A kind of brinjal; Solanum diffusum , Wat.
s. m. 1. Name of a warlike musical instrument, a kettledrum. 2. (dakh.) Two antelope horns in opposite directions joined at their bases, carried by faḳīrs (Qanoon-e-islam).
s. m. Name of a rāg or musical mode.
adj. in compos. Smiting, striking; striker, killer.

subs: maru-bhūmi, s.f., or maru-deś, maru-sthal, s.m. A dry or desert tract of country, arid region; barren soil.

ety: pass. part. of عرف 'to know'
def: part. adj. Known, well-known; famous, noted, celebrated, notorious; — (in Gram.) active (voice); — s.m. The active voice (of a verb); — (in Alg.) a known quantity; — the letters و and ي are said to be maʻrūf when the former has the sound of ū in nūr, and the latter that of ī in pīr.
more: adj. عرف knowing. Notorious; celebrated ( mashhūr ).
n. m. 1. The active voice. 2. The letters, و & ي are said to be mārūf when the former has the sound of ū and the latter of ī , as in nūr light, nīr water. 3. ( Alg . S. ज्ञात राशि ) A known quantity.

(part. pass. of عرف) adj. 1. Known, notorious, noted, celebrated. 2. Active (voice of a verb). 3. The letters و and ي are said to be maﻌrūf, when the former has the sound of ū and the latter of ī; as in نور nūr, Splendour, and لڙکي laṛkī, A girl.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَعْرُوف (ma'rūf), borrowed from Arabic مَعْرُوف (maʕrūf), from عَرَفَ (ʕarafa).

def: s.m. Name of an angel (see s.v. hārūt). s.m. Name of an angel (he, together with another, named Mārūt, severely censured mankind before the throne of God; they were, therefore, both sent down to earth in human shapes to judge of the temptations to which man was subject. They could not withstand them; they were seduced by women, and committed every species of iniquity, for which they were suspended by the feet in a well in Babylon, where they are to remain in great torment till the day of judgment. — See the Qorʼān, Sūra 2; Sale's Transl. p. 20).
s.m. Wind, air, breath; — god of the wind (one of a number of deities who are guardians of the North-west quarter)
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit मृत (mṛta). Doublet of مردہ (murdā).

ety: fr. maruta
def: adj. Of or belonging to the Maruts; proceeding from the Maruts; belonging to air or wind, &c.; — s.m. Air, wind; vital air, one of the three humours of the body; — a Marut; — burnt offering on the occasion of conception, &c.
s.m. 'Son of the Maruts, or son of the Wind'; a patronymic of Bhīm-sen; and of Hanumān.

ety: S. मड़कः, with w unserted

more: n. m. S. मरुवक Sweet marjoram; Origanum majoranā . (?)
s. m. 1. Name of a strong scented plant. (Artemisia vulgaris). 2. Ocimum pilosum: Roxb.

ety: doub. caus. of marnā
def: v.t. To cause to be killed, to cause to be put to death; — (doub. caus. of mārnā), v.t. To cause to be beaten, &c.; — to suffer sodomy (the word gāṅṛ being usually expressed)
more: v. a. Caus. of مارنا q. v. To suffer sodomy or improper sexual intercourse.
n. m. A cess taken on marriages. Carnegy .
(causal of مرنا) v. a. To cause to be beaten, to cause to be killed.
subs: v.t. (intens.) To cause to be put to death; — to dismiss a charge or complaint.
ex: You would've got me killed today for sure!
wety: Causative form of مرنا (marnā) with stem augmented by وا (vā)

ety: pass. part. of رضي (orig. رضو 'to be pleased,' &c.
def: part. adj. Found pleasing, well-pleasing; liked, approved; agreeable, acceptable (syn. pasandīda); in which one takes a pleasure; — taking favourably; — s.f. Anything in which one takes a pleasure; — pleasure; assent, consent; choice; will, volition
more: n. f. رضو was pleased. 1. Assent; concurrence (رضا 1). 2. Choice; volition (اختيار 1, 2). marzī ke muāfiq, marzī muāfiq , adv. Satisfactorily ( khātir-khuāh ). marzī milnā, yā paṭnā , v. n. To agree; to be of one mind; to suit. Marzī mile kā saudā hai. marzī meṅ ānā , v. n. To like; approve ( achchhā lagnā , 1). apnī marzī, yā khushī se . adv. Voluntarily.
(from رضو Heb. רצה ) 1. adj. Agreeable, acceptable. 2. Taking favourably. 3. s. f. Will, assent, consent, pleasure. (Plur. مرضيات). ¶ marẓ-ī (from مرض maraẓ ) adj. (dakh.) Sick. ( Harris ).
subs: or marẕī-muwāfiq, adv. To the will or pleasure (of, -kī), satisfactorily
or marẓī paṭnā, (-kī), To agree, be of one mind; to suit
(-kī), To meet the wishes (of), to prove agreeable (to); to like
adv. Of (one's own) free will and consent, voluntarily.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَرْضِی (marzī), from Arabic مُرْضِي (murḍī, “pleasing”).

ety: Prk. मासं; S. मांसं (Vedic मास्)
def: s.m. Flesh, meat
more: n. m. S. मास; Pr. mansam ; Port. mes ; P. māh . Lat. mensum ; Eng. moon. A month. 1. Jit kit ghū ghū rākhe bās, ūjar chāhe bārā mās . Rus. Prov. (Said of bad men). Wherever owls to dwell retire all through the year they wish for ruin dire. 2. Ek mās ritu āge dhāve . Prov. The season runs a month ahead. (Its character is seen a month previous). mās-kabār , n. A monthly statement. mās nimās, lit . month by month. Interest added to capital, and subject to compound interest.

n. m. S. मांस Pālī, māso . Flesh (گوشت) 1. Hāṛ hoṅge to mās ho rahegā . Prov. If the bones are there the flesh will come. (If he only lives he will grow strong again). 2. Jūṅ jūṅ kosūṅ deh baḍhat saë, til bhar mās ghaṭe nāhīṅ, kosat bhī sās mare nāhīṅ . Rus. Wom. I curse and curse and she grows all the sleeker, Not by one grain of flesh is she the weaker, For all my cursing death won't come to seek her. mās-ādhārī , adj. Flesh-eater ( gosht-khuār ). mās nochnā , To claw; mangle.
s. m. 1. (Sans. मास Port. mes ) A month. ماسانت मासांत māsānt, s. f. The last day of the solar or lunar month. 2. (for मांस ) Flesh, meat.

more: v. n. 1. To tear; to be torn, rent. 1. Āj kāl nar hai joban ke mad pe, kahīṅ masak le na mohe, naī sī jāṅch! Holi. Mauj. The season this when lust on young men's strong, Their new bloom spied my breasts they 'll press erelong. 2. Chalo haule haule! dhamak se ni-bakhto! Gaī sab masak merī cholī, kahāro! Wom. Rangīn. Go slow, d' you hear, you wretches, slow! Torn all my boddice, bearers, so! 2. To move; stir; move from its place. Us se maskā to jātā hī nahīṅ . He cannot even move. (He is so lazy). 3. To press, squeeze, or hold one tight. 1. Dhar maskā, rāt marī hotī! Wom. song. He squeezed me so last night, I nearly died. 2. Jab terī akal ṭhīk hoëgī, jab jāëgī terī chhātī maskī, Kajrā mat deo, baras das kī! Wom. song. When you to woman's state are grown Squeezed then your breasts full-blown you 'll own, At ten years eye-paint's all too soon. maskā maskī , Pinching and squeezing. maskor'ā ,…
v. n. To be torn, split, rent, burst.

ety: masak˚ prob. = S. मष+कृ
def: v.n. To be torn or rent; to tear; to split, burst; to fall asunder, or in pieces; — to be very tight (a dress)

ety: masak˚ = S. मस्क(ते), rt. मस्क्
def: v.n. To move, stir.

ety: v.n. of مثل 'to compare, liken'
def: s.f. Fable, tale; parable, apologue; proverb, adage, proverbial saying; — example, instance, case in point; — comparison, simile, allegory, metaphor; — post, station; department
more: n. f. A proverb; proverbial saying ( kahāvat ). gaṅvār maslah , n. m. 1. A rustic proverb. 2. A plain saying. wohī masal hai . As the proverb says.
(Heb. ) s. f. 1. Fable, allegory, parable simile, metaphor, proverb, apologue. 2. Post, station, department, an example. ¶ mis̤l, s. f. Similitude, likeness, like.
subs: To bring a proverb into use, to apply or to employ a proverb; — to adduce as an example

def: s.f. corr. of مثل mas̤al, q.v. s.m. Flesh, meat
s.f. corr. of مشعل mashʻal, q.v. s.f. A sort of cresset; a torch, flambeau; — a lantern
subs: To show a torch, to hold a torch before or near (a person); to carry a lighted torch before (a person)

def: adv. = mas̤alaṉ, q.v. s.m. Flesh, meat
s.m. Materials, ingredients, &c. (corr. of مصالح maṣālěḥ, q.v.).
s.m. = مسله masla, q.v. s.m. (dialec.) Sample, specimen; pattern, model; — fashion, shape, make, build, construction.
more: n. m. 1. A maxim; saw; an aphorism. 2. A tenet; doctrine. 3. A proposition. maslah baghārnā , v. a. ( Ironic .) To preach; cant. masle-dāṅ , n. m. One versed in religious doctrines.

def: s.m. corr. of next, q.v.

ety: for A. مثلة mas̤alat, n. of un. fr. mas̤al
def: s.m. = mas̤al, q.v.
subs: A rustic proverb; a plain saying.

ety: acc. of mas̤al, q.v.
def: adv. For example, for instance, e.g.; namely.
more: adv. For example; e. g.; for instance; as; namely.
adv. For example, e. g., namely.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَثَلاً (masalan), from Arabic مَثَلًا (maṯalan).

ety: for A. مسألة
def: s.f. = masala or masla, q.v. s.m. = mas̤al, q.v.
subs: A rustic proverb; a plain saying.

def: s. f. See مسله masla.

s.m. (pl. of maṣlaḥat, q.v.), lit. 'Things for the good of; or things or affairs conducive to good'; affairs; — employments, occupations; — honour, glory; — materials, ingredients, necessaries (as for cooking, building, &c.); spicery, spices; condiments, seasoning; drug or drugs; materials used to give a finish to a garment, &c.; edging, hem, border; gold or silver lace; — maṣālěḥ-dār, vulg. masāle-dār, adj. & s.m. Seasoned with spices; — bound or edged with lace, &c.; — cloth worked over, or edged, with gold or silver lace, &c.; — one who supplies materials (for a building, &c.)
more: n. m. 1. Materials (اسباب 2). 2. Ingredients; spices; seasoning. Chane haiṅ, masāle ke bane haiṅ, ghī ke tale haiṅ, nūn mirach ke mile haiṅ! Lucknow Street cry. 3. Gold or silver lace. 1. Ṭopī yā angarkhe kā masālā . 2. Aë jān! aisā chhātī se lipṭāyā bhīch-kar, Angyā kā merī sārā masālā masal gayā . Wom. Jān Sahab. My dear! he pressed me to his breast so tight, The lace upon my boddice he crushed quite. 4. ( Slang ) A young woman. Ek aur masālah āyā hai. masāle-dār , n. m. One who supplies materials for a building, etc. masāle-dār , adj. 1. Seasoned with spices. 2. Bound with lace. masālah ḍālnā , v. n. To season.

(plur. of مصلحت from صلح Heb. Chald. Aram. ) s. m. 1. Affairs. 2. Honour, glory, employments. 3. Materials. 4. (Hind.) Drugs, spices, seasoning. مصالح ڐالنا maṣāliḥ ḍālnā, or مصالح دينا maṣāliḥ denā, To season.
subs: or maṣālěḥ ḍālnā (-meṅ), To put condiments or seasoning (into), to season.

def: s.m. pl. (of maslak, q.v.), Ways, paths.

ety: masal˚, fr. S. मृश or म्रक्ष, with H. caus. augment al = āl = lā
def: v.t. To rub to pieces; to crush between the palms; to bruise, crush, pulverize, triturate; — to press, squeeze
more: v. a. S. मक्षण 1. To crush between the palms; triturate. 2. To press; squeeze. maslā daslī, malā dalī , n. f. Pressing and squeezing. Maslā daslī meṅ aṅgiyā masak gaī re! Wom.
v. a. To bruise, to crush, to break with the hand.

ety: pl. of masamm, n. of place fr. سمّ 'to pierce, to pass through'; — but used as a sing. in Persian and Urdu
def: s.m. 'Place of passing through,' perforation, or pore (of the body; — pl. masāmmāt).
more: n. m. pl. masāmāt . سم A pore of the skin.
(from سمّ) s. m. A pore (of the body). (Plur. مسامات masāmāt ).

def: s.m. The medicinal seed Vernonia (or Serratula) anthelminthica.

ety: Prk. मसाणं; S. श्मशानं
def: s.m. A place where the dead are burnt, crematory, ustrinum (syn. marghaṭ); a cemetery; — a demon, an evil spirit (said to have influence on children); — a disease to which children are liable, possession by an evil spirit; convulsions; — whooping-cough
more: n. m. S. श्मशान, Pr. masānam 1. See مرگهتَ (1). 2. An evil spirit said to have an evil influence on children. 3. ( masān kā rog, sūkhiyā masān ) The whooping cough.
s. m. A place where a dead body is burnt or buried, a cemetery.
subs: or sūkhiyā masān, s.m. The whooping-cough.

def: s.m. A species of medicinal plant or fruit, Serratula anthelmintica.
s.m. = مسان masān, q.v. s.m. The medicinal seed Vernonia (or Serratula) anthelminthica.

ety: for A. مثانة mas̤ānat
def: s.m. The bladder
more: n. m. The bladder. zof-i-masānā . Weakness of the bladder; inability to hold in the urine.
subs: s.m. Weakness of the bladder; inability to retain the urine.

ety: masān, q.v. + Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. One of the (seven) goddesses of small-pox, measles, scarlatina, whooping-cough, &c.
more: W. n. f. One of the seven sister goddesses of small-pox, measles, scarletina, etc.

ety: masān, q.v.+Prk. इओ = S. इकः
def: s.m. One who removes the remains of a burnt corpse; — one who removes the influence of an evil spirit.
more: n. m. 1. One who removes the remains of the burnt corpse. 2. One who dispels the influence of an evil spirit.

def: s.m. See s.v. mās, 'month.' s.m. Flesh, meat
s.m. A sapphire; — an emerald.

r: maṣārif, maṣraf, maṣārif-ě-be-jā, maṣārif-ě-shādī, maṣārif-ě-ẓururī, maṣārif-ě-mulk
def: s.m. pl. (of maṣraf, q.v.), Expenses, disbursements; costs; charges; uses; — changes, vicissitudes
more: n. m. pl. of صرف Expenses. masārif-i-bejā , Unnecessary expenses. masārif-i-shādī , Marriage expenses. masārif-i-zurūrī , n. m. Immediate or necessary expenses. masārif-i-ārzī, yā gair-muqarrarī , n. m. See kharch-i-gair-māmūlī. masārif-i-mulk , State expenditure.

ety: for A. مسرة, inf. n. of سَرّ 'to rejoice, to make glad or happy'
def: s.f. A cause of happiness, or joy, or gladness; — joy, happiness, gladness, cheerfulness, liveliness; rapture, delight.
more: (inf. of سرّ) s. f. Gladness, cheerfulness, joy.

def: s.f. (dialec.) = mashṭ, q.v. s.f. Silence
subs: v.n. To remain silent and still; to feign sleep

def: s.m.f. A kind of bird.
n. m. Pl. of مسئله q. v. 1. Propositions; problems. 2. The precepts of Mahomed.
more: s. plur. (of مسأله or مسله) 1. Questions. 2. The precepts of Muhammad, demands, propositions, problems.

ety: S. मांस+वृद्धिः
def: s.m. A fleshy excrescence or swelling; a wart (syn. massā or masā, q.v.).
more: s. m. A wart, a fleshy excrescence or swelling.

ety: pass. part. of ثبت 'to remain fixed,' &c.
def: adj. Fixed, firm.
more: (part. pass. of ثبت) adj. Firm, fixed.

ety: n. of place fr. صدر 'to return, or go back'
def: s.m. 'Place whence one returns, or goes, or turns away'; place of issuing or proceeding; origin, source, spring, theme; (in Gram.) the root (of a word) from which proceed the derivatives of verbs, an infinitive noun, nomen verbi, or nomen actionis.
more: n. m. صدر 1. Source; origin. 2. ( Gram .) The infinitive mood.
(noun of place of صدر) s. m. 1. Source, origin, spring, theme. 2. The infinitive or noun of action (in grammar). 3. A place. (Plur. مصادر).

def: adj. Of or relating to the maṣdar or infinitive; having the sense of the verbal noun; — belonging to a source.

ety: pass. part. of سدّ 'to close, stop,' &c.
def: part. Closed, closed up, shut, stopped, obstructed
more: adj. Obstructed; closed. masdūdh. , v. n. 1. To fail; cease. 2. To be obstructed.
(part. pass. of سدّ) adj. Obstructed, closed, shut.
subs: v.n. To be closed, or stopped, or obstructed; — to stop, cease, fail.

ety: pass. part. of صدر 'to return, or go back,' &c.
def: part. Issued; derived; derivative.
more: (part. pass. from صدر) adj. Derived, derivative ( Harris ).

ety: for A. مصدورة maṣdūrat, fem. of maṣdūr
def: part. = maṣdūr, q.v. part. Issued; derived; derivative.

ety: pl. of mas (not in use) = Prk. मस्स = S. श्मश्रु
def: s.f. pl. Hair or down on the face which appears in early youth
more: s. f. plur. Down, small hairs on the lips before the beard grows.
subs: The hair of the whiskers, &c. to begin to sprout.

def: n. f. The down on the lips of youth. Maseṅ bhīgnā .
adj. corr. of مشغول mashg̠ūl, q.v.

ety: S. माशः
def: s.m. (dialec.) Anger.
more: Mah. n. m. 1. Pulse (آرَو). Māsh kī dāl . 2. A bean belonging to the hewant harvest; phaseolus radiatus . māsh mārnā , v. a. To charm; bind by a spell. Mil gaī gar koī Bangāle kī aubāsh tumheṅ Bheṛ ban-jāoge, māregī jo do māsh tumheṅ . Wom. Jān Sāhab. If you perchance some Bengal wanton e'er should meet With two pea grains she 'll turn you to a sheep complete. māshī-raṅg , The color of uṛad , q. v.
s. m. A kind of vetch. (Phaseolus max. or radiatus).

ety: inf. n. of مسح 'to wipe,' &c.
def: s.m. Wiping; drawing the hand over the surface (of anything, in order to wipe it); handling, stroking, rubbing; washing, cleaning; anointing; the wiping (the neck, &c.) which forms part of the purificatory rites observed by Muslims before engaging in prayer; — perambulation; travelling.
more: Mah. n. m. سحي scraped off. Wetting the forehead, ears, and neck, a part of wuzu , q. v.
(Heb. ) s. m. 1. Drawing the hand over, wiping, washing, cleaning, anointing, a part of the operation of purifying before prayer. 2. Perambulating, travelling.

ety: S. माषकः
def: s.m. A kind of bean, &c. ( = māsh, q.v.); — a jeweller's or goldsmith's weight, equal to eight rattīs or seeds of the Abrus precatorius (it is sometimes, however, reckoned = ten rattīs, and sometimes = five; the weight in common use is about 17 grains Troy); — four annas (in the language of brokers); — a small nail
subs: The weight of a māshā, full eight rattis
v.n. To be one moment a māshā the next a tolā; to be very changeable, fickle, or inconstant.

def: s.m. A weight of eight rattīs, &c. ( = māshā, q.v.). s.m. A kind of bean, Phaseolus radiatus; — a kind of pulse (having seeds marked with black and grey spots; — in Hindī, the name is given to mūng; in Urdū, to mūṅg and to uṛad); a cutaneous eruption resembling beans; — a fool, blockhead
more: n. m. S. माष 1. A weight equal to 1/12th of a tolah or 8 rattīs , q. v. Koī māshā, koī tolā . 2. ( Brokers ) Four annas. 3. A small nail. māshā bhar . Full 8 rattīs . māshā tolah h. , v. n. To be changeable, inconstant. Māshā ghaṛī meṅ tolah hai zar-dār kā mizāj . The temper of a moneyed man such changes knows, A grain weight now, an ounce in half an hour it shows.
s. m. Name of a small weight consisting of eight rattīs, q. v.
subs: s.f. A kind of leguminous shrub, Glycine debilis
(-ko), To throw beans at; to charm, to bind by a spell.

def: intj. God preserve him or it from the evil eye!

s.m. A kind of bean, Phaseolus radiatus; — a kind of pulse (having seeds marked with black and grey spots; — in Hindī, the name is given to mūng; in Urdū, to mūṅg and to uṛad); a cutaneous eruption resembling beans; — a fool, blockhead
subs: s.f. A kind of leguminous shrub, Glycine debilis
(-ko), To throw beans at; to charm, to bind by a spell.

def: s.m. pl. (of shabah, q.v.), Likenesses, resemblances, semblances; forms, figures, pictures, images.
more: (part. act. III. of شبه) 1. s. m. Resemblance, picture, similitude. 2. Probability. 3. adj. Like, resembling, analogous. مشابه به mushābih-bi-hi, Likened to it, in comparison with it.

def: s.m. pl. (of mashg̠ala, q.v.), Employments, occupations, duties (of a house, &c.).
n. m. شـﮩد being present. 1. Sight; vision. 2. Observation; ocular demonstration.
(pl. of mashhūr, q.v.), adj. & s.m. pl. Renowned, famous, celebrated, notorious; — renowned or famous men; celebrities.
n. m. Pl. of شيخ an old man. Holy men; patriarchs; divines; dervises.
more: s. plur. (of شيخ) 1. Old men. 2. Holy persons, dervises, adorers. 3. (as a singular) A patriarch, a divine.

def: s.m. pl. (of shaiḵẖ, q.v.), Old or aged men; shaiḵẖs; elders; holy persons; doctors; patriarchs; dervishes; — (as a sing.) a holy man; a patriarch, &c.

ety: mashāʼiḵẖ+āna = Zend āna or ana = S. ana
def: adj. & adv. Of or relating to holy persons, &c.; — patriarchal; — in the manner of holy men; as a spiritual director.
s.f. Holiness, sanctity, piety.
s. f. Holiness, sanctity, piety.

s.m. A bean, &c. ( = māshā, q.v.). s.m. A kind of bean, Phaseolus radiatus; — a kind of pulse (having seeds marked with black and grey spots; — in Hindī, the name is given to mūng; in Urdū, to mūṅg and to uṛad); a cutaneous eruption resembling beans; — a fool, blockhead
subs: s.f. A kind of leguminous shrub, Glycine debilis
(-ko), To throw beans at; to charm, to bind by a spell.

ety: for A. مشقة, inf. n. of شقّ 'to distress; to be distressing; to be difficult,' &c.
def: s.f. Inconvenience, trouble, labour, pains, toil, assiduity; distress, pain, affliction, misfortune
more: n. f. شق Labor (محنت 1). mashaqqat-i-shadīd , Hard labour. bā-mashaqqat , With labor. bā-mashaqqat va jaulāṅ , With labor in irons. bilā-mashaqqat , Without labor.
(inf. of شقّ) s. f. Trouble, labour, pains, toil, assiduity.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مشقت (mašaqqat), itself from Arabic مَشَقَّة (mašaqqa).

ety: n. of place fr. شعل 'to burn,' &c.
def: s.f. A sort of cresset; a torch, flambeau; — a lantern
more: n. f. A torch; flambeau. mashal jalānā , v. a. To light a torch. mashal dikhānā , v. a. To carry a lighted torch. mashal le-kar ḍhūṅḍnā , v. a. See chirāg/?/ le-kar ḍhūṅḍnā , under chirāg/?/ .
s. f. (from شعل) A torch, flambeau, lantern. مشعلچي mashʼaﻌl-chī, s. m. A torchbearer.
subs: To show a torch, to hold a torch before or near (a person); to carry a lighted torch before (a person)
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مَشْعَل (mašʕal).

ety: for A. مشعلة mashʻalat
def: s.m. = mashʻal, q.v. s.f. A sort of cresset; a torch, flambeau; — a lantern
more: s. m. (from شعل) A torch, flambeau, lantern. مشعلچي mashʼaﻌl-chī, s. m. A torchbearer.
subs: To show a torch, to hold a torch before or near (a person); to carry a lighted torch before (a person)

def: s.m. A bearer of the mashʻal, a torch-bearer; — (in India, and in the households of Europeans) a scullion.
more: n. m. 1. (Fac. andherchī ) A torch-bearer. 1. Mashalchī andhā hotā hai . Prov. The torch-bearer cannot see his own way. 2. Mashalchī mare to pat-bījnā bane; yahāṅ bhī chamke, wahāṅ bhī chamke . Players. Bhāṅḍs . The dead torch-bearer to a glow worm turns, On earth he burns and in the next world burns. 3. Ab se bhīk māṅgne jāeṅge to ek mashalchī naukar rakkheṅge . When I go a begging again I'll entertain a torch-bearer. (Said by a blind beggar). 2. ( Europeans .) A scullion.

ety: pl. of mishamm, n. of instr.fr. شمّ 'to smell'
def: s.m. (lit. 'noses'), The smell, the sense of smelling.
more: (from شمّ Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. The smell, the sense of smelling.

ety: v.n. fr. مشر
def: adj. & s.m. Going (or one who goes) with a graceful, sprightly air.
more: n. m. mishrānī, misrānī , n. f. 1. A title of Maithila Brahmins . 2. A form of address to Brahmins. Āiye, mishshar jī! 3. ( Arith. mishr ) Compound. mishr joṛ . Compound addition.

s. m. 1. A title of Brāhmaṇs, who were brought to India from Sākadwīp by Kr̤ish̤ṇa in order to cure the leprosy of his son Sāmba: also, a title of some Brāhmaṇs from Mithilā, &c. 2. A Hindū physician.

ety: n. of place, or instr. fr. شعر 'to know,' &c.
def: s.m. External sense (as hearing or seeing, &c.).
more: part. act. (IV. of شعر) Signifying, denoting, indicating, bearing the marks of. ¶ mashʼaﻌr or mishʼaﻌr, s. A sense (i. e. one of the five senses).

ety: fr. عشرة ʻasharat, 'ten'
def: s.m. A company, troop, collective body (of people), a community, society (ʻso called because they are many').
more: (noun of place from عشر Heb. and Chald. ) s. m. A company, a troop, society. ¶ muʼaﻌshshar (part. pass. II. of عشر) adj. Composed of or relating to ten; a decagon.

def: s.f. (dialec.) = masahrī, q.v. s.f. A bed-curtain for protection against musquitoes, musquito-curtain.

ety: for A. مشعرة mashʻarat; see mashʻar
def: s.m. A place where poets meet to recite their verses, &c.; a rendezvous of poets.
more: (noun of place of شعر Heb. ) s. m. A place where poets meet to read their verses, &c.

def: s.f. See مسہري masahrī.

ety: mashfiq+āna = Zend āna or ana = S. ana
def: adv. Affectionately, kindly; in a friendly manner.

ety: for A. مشغلة mashg̠alat, v.n. fr. شغل 'to busy,' &c.
def: s.m. 'A thing that causes one to be busied,' &c.; business, occupation, employment; — pastime; diversion.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مشغله (mašġala), from Arabic مَشْغَلة (mašḡala), from the root ش غ ل (š ḡ l).

ety: pass. part. of شغل 'to busy, occupy,' &c.
def: part. Busied, busy, occup ed, employed, engaged (in); wholly dedicated (to), diligent; anxious (about)
more: adj. شغل Employed; occupied; engaged; busy. mashgūl h. , v. n. To engage; occupy.
(part. pass. of شغل) adj. Employed, occupied, diligent, anxious, busy.
subs: (-meṅ, or -se), To be busied or busy (with), be occupied (with); to engage (in), &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَشْغُول (mašġūl), from Arabic مَشْغُول (mašḡūl). Compare Punjabi مَشْغُول (maśġūl) / ਮਸ਼ਗ਼ੂਲ (maśġūl), Sindhi مَشْغُول (maśġūl) / मशग़ूल (maśġūl), Gujarati મશગૂલ (maśgūl), Bengali মশগুল (mośgul).

def: s.m. corr. of mashg̠ala, q.v.
more: (corrupt. for مشغله mashg̠ẖala ) s. m. Employment, occupation, pastime.

ety: fr. mashg̠ūl
def: s.f. Occupation, employment (i.q. mashg̠ala),

ety: n. of place fr. شہد 'to bear witness or testimony' (to
def: s.f. A place where a martyr has died, or is buried; a place of martyrdom; a place of religious visitation; a tomb; — a place of assembling, an assembly.
more: (noun of place from شہد Heb. Ch. ) s. f. A place of martyrdom, a place of assembly, a tomb.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مَشْهَد (mašhad).

ety: pass. part. of شہد 'to bear witness or testimony'
def: part. adj. Witnessed; attested; proved; evinced; beheld; — clear, manifest; — s.m. Friday; — the day of resurrection.
more: (part. pass. of شہد Chald. Heb. ) adj. Attested, proved, evinced, beheld, clear, manifest.

def: adj. شـﮩر Well-known mashhūr o mārūf Noted; famous ( parghaṭ ). mashhūr bad-dyānatī , n. f. Notorious corruption. mashhūr k. , v. a. To proclaim ( ishtehār denā ). mashhūr h. , v. n. 1. To become public. 2. To become notorious, famous. mashhūr hai , It is currently reported.

ety: pass. part. of شہر 'to make conspicuous, or notorious,' &c.
def: part. adj. Well-known, celebrated, notable, noted, famous, notorious; apparent, conspicuous, manifest, public, published, given out, proclaimed, divulged, reported. — mashhūr karnā or kar-denā, v.t. To give out, publish, proclaim
more: (part. pass. of شہر) adj. Celebrated, published, divulged, conspicuous, famous, well-known, noted, notorious. مشہورکرنا mashʼhūr karnā, To publish, to proclaim.

(-par-se), To knock off a fly (from a thing or place, with a ball or an arrow), to be a good marksman
s.m. lit. 'One who would suck a fly rather than lose any of the food or drink into which it fell'; a miser, niggard, skinflint
s.m. One who kills a fly or flies; — a dirty, fllthy fellow; an idler, a lazy fellow; — one who makes an exact copy of a document, &c., mistakes, and blots, &c. included
To kill flies; to be unemployed, to idle, to trifle, to fool (syn. makkhī uṛānā)
(-par), Flies to buzz (about), or to sit (upon); — to be disgusting, or ugly, &c.
To swallow a living fly; — to bring evil upon oneself; — to tell great lies
Not to allow a fly to sit on the nose; — to shrink from incurring an obligation.

def: s.f. Piercing suddenly (with a spear); — striking, beating, or lashing with velocity; — tearing; — writing, drawing (the letters); — combing; — chafing, or galling the skin (a garment, &c.); — drawing (a garment, or a thong, &c.) every way in order to stretch it; — eating up the best parts (of forage); — a model (for imitation), a copy (to write after); an example; an exercise; — practice, usage, use; — adj. Galled between the thighs (a man); — tall, slender (girl)

s. f. 1. Piercing suddenly with a spear, striking with velocity. 2. Beating, lashing, tearing. 3. Combing. 4. Drawing (a garment, thing, &c.) every way in order to stretch it. 5. Eating of the best parts (of forage, &c.). 6. (A man) galled between the thighs. 7. Tall, slender (girl). 8. ( met. ) An example, copy to write after, exercise, practice, usage, use.
subs: s.m. Anything placed under a sheet of paper that one may write commodiously; a paste-board, a pad.

def: s.f. A place or house of prayer, a Musllm temple, a mosque
more: n. f. سجد bowed his head. A mosque. 1. Take ke vāste masjid ḍhāte haiṅ! Prov. For two pice the mosque is destroyed. 2. Masjid ḍhaë gaī, mehrāb rah gaī . Prov. The mosque destroyed the arch remains. (The substance gone the traces but remain). 3. Masjid o but-khānah saṅg o khisht se donoṅ bane, Farq is meṅ kuchh nahīṅ, tāmīr hai donoṅ kī ek . Mosque and pagoda with the selfsame stones and bricks are laid, No difference there is, both structures similarly made. 4. Masjid jāoṅ, na ṭhākur duārā, Hai ghaṭ hī meṅ khojan-hārā . Kabir. Nor mosque nor temple's aught to me, He whom I seek in me I see. 5. Sāligrām, na mahjat koī; nā whāṅ sunnat, janeu hoī . (The next world.) Kabīr. Nor holy stone, nor mosque is there; no circumcision and no sacred string whate'er. masjid-abābīl . The red-rumped mosque swallow; Hirundo Daurica . masjid meṅ īṅṭ ulaṭ-kar rakhnā , Mah. Wom. v. a. To place a brick upside…
(noun of place from سجد Heb. and Aram. ) s. f. A mosque. مسجد جامع masjid-i-jāmīﻌ, A great mosque, a cathedral.
subs: s.m. & f. The mosque-swallow, Hirundo daurica
or masjid-ě-jāměʻ, s.f. The congregational mosque, the principal mosque (of a town or city).
ex: اس عبادت گاہ کو بنی اسرائیل مسجد بھی کہا جاتا تھا۔
karācī meṉ muqīm yahūdiyoṉ kī tārīx
This place of worship also used to be known as the Bene Israel synagogue.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَسْجِد (masjid), borrowed from Arabic مَسْجِد (masjid), from سَجَدَ (sajada). First attested in c. 1609 as Middle Hindi مسجد (msjd /⁠masjid⁠/). Displaced Urdu مَسِیت (masīt).

ety: rel. n. fr. masjid
def: adj. Of or belonging to a mosque (a person or thing).
more: adj. Of the mosque (person or thing).

ety: pass. part. of سجد 'to prostrate one self in adoration or worship'
def: part. Adored, worshipped.
more: (part. pass. of سجد Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Worshipped, adored.

ety: = S. म्रक्ष
def: s.m. Butter (syn. makkhan).
more: n. m. S. मक्षण Butter ( makkhan ).

ety: fr. masaknā, q.v.
def: s.f. Pinching and squeezing.
(corrupt. from Portug. mes, A month, and acabar, To end, to terminate) The last day of a month.

ety: n. of place fr. سکن 'to inhabit,' &c.
def: s.m. Place of habitation; habitation, abode, dwelling, house.
more: n. m. سکن rested. A dwelling ( bās ). maskan-i-māmūlī , n. m. Ordinary residence.
(noun of place of سکن Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. An abode, a dwelling, habitation. ¶ musakkin (part. act. II.) Anodyne.

ety: caus. of masaknā, q.v.
def: v.t. To tear, rend; to split, burst; to clutch tightly, to press, squeeze ( = masaknā).

ety: for A. مسکنة, fr. sukūn, inf. n. of سکن 'to be or become still or motionless,' &c.
def: s.f. Lowliness, humility, submissiveness (cf. miskīn); lowness, abjectness; abasement, humiliation; poverty, indigence, wretchedness, misery.
more: (from سکن) s. f. Humility, poverty, misery.

def: s.m. A religious mendicant, a Brāhman in the fourth order (who carries a bamboo cane); — the moon.
s.m. A religious mendicant, a Brāhman in the fourth order (who carries a bamboo cane); — the moon.

ety: n. of place fr. سقط 'to fall, drop,' &c.
def: s.m. Place of falling; birthplace; — the town of Mascat (in Arabia)
subs: s.m. Place of birth, native country.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مَسْقَط (masqaṭ).

def: s.m. (dialec.) corr. of mashkīza, q.v. s.m. A small mashk or leather water-bag; a small leather water-bottle.

ety: inf. n. of مسخ 'to change, to metamorphose,' &c.
def: s.m. Changing, metamorphosing into a more deformed shape (as men into apes); — adopting the thoughts of another, but changing some of his words; plagiarism; — emaciating; galling (the back of an animal) by excessive labour; — adj. Changed into a meaner form, metamorphosed; ugly, misshapen
more: s. m. Changing, metamorphosing from a superiour to an inferiour shape. مسخ هونا masḵẖ honā, To be changed, to be metamorphosed into an inferiour shape (as, from man to beast).
subs: v.n. To be changed into a meaner form, to be metamorphosed.

def: s.m. corr. of مسخره masḵẖara, q.v.

ety: for A. مسخرة masḵẖarat, v.n. fr. سخر 'to mock, scoff, laugh (at
def: s.m. One who mocks, or laughs (at); a buffoon, fool, jester, droll, wag, facetious fellow; — adj. Jocular, facetious, droll.
more: adj. سخر laughed at. Jocular; droll ( ṭhaṭol ). maskharah ; Illit. maskharī-bāz , n. m. A jester; a buffoon ( ṭhaṭol ). maskharah-pan; maskharī ; P. maskharagī , n. m. Jesting; drollery; buffoonery ( ṭhaṭolī ). 1. Julāhe kī maskharī māṅ bahan se! Prov. The weaver cracks indecent jokes with his mother and sister! 2. Bharī ghare devar, bhatāre maskharī . Bhoj. Prov. Of younger brothers-in-law a housefull, yet with her husband she must jest. maskhar-ul-lazī , n. m. A foolish stupid person. maskharī k. , Illit. v. a. To jest; joke. maskharī meṅ ḍālnā , v. n. To put off with a joke.
(from سخر) s. m. A droll, a jester, a buffoon, a laughingstock.

ety: ˚pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: Jesting, jest; drollery, buffoonery, fun.
more: s. f. Buffoonery, drollery, jesting, jest, derision.

ety: ˚pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: Jesting, jest; drollery, buffoonery, fun.
s.f. = masḵẖarāpan, q.v.
subs: To make jest, or fun; to jest, joke, &c.
or masḵẖarī-meṅ ḍālnā, v.t. To turn or put off with a joke.

ety: masak(nā) + orā = Prk. उल्लओ = S. उल+कः
def: s.m. A move or turn on one side (in sleeping; syn. karwaṭ)
subs: or maskorā mārnā, To turn over on the side (in sleeping).

ety: pass. part. of صقل 'to polish'
def: part. Polished, furbished.
more: (part. pass. of صقل) adj. Polished, furbished.

ety: pass. part. of سکن 'to inhabit'
def: part. adj. Inhabited; habitable.
more: (part. pass. of سکن) adj. Inhabited, habitable.

ety: for A. مسألة maśalat, inf. n. of سأل 't ask,' &c., used tropically in the sense of a pass. part. n.
def: s.m. 'A thing asked,' a question; a proposition, problem; a matter, or affair, proposed for decision or determination; a precept (esp. of Moḥammad); tenet, doctrine; an aphorism, a maxim, saw
more: (for مسأله from سأل Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. Question, proposition, problem, a precept of Muḥammad.
subs: vulg. masle-dāṅ, s.m. One versed in precepts, or doctrines, &c.

ety: fr. masalnā, q.v.
def: s.m. Bruising, crushing; pressing, squeezing

ety: prob. corr. fr. A. مشلة mas̤lat; P. mas̤la
def: s.m. (dialec.) Sample, specimen; pattern, model; — fashion, shape, make, build, construction.

ety: for A. مصلحة, inf. n. of صلح 'to be good, or right,' &c.
def: s.f. A cause, means, or occasion, of good; a thing or affair conducive to good, or that is for good; a thing that is good and right; welfare; that which is best, a prudent measure, advisable thing; advisability, sound or good policy; an urgent occasion; — convenience, expedience; an expedient; fitness; — advice, counsel; — an affair, business; subject
more: n. f. 1. Expedience; advisability. 2. Good policy. Har kase maslahat-i-khesh niko mīdānad . P. Prov. Every one knows his own interest best. maslahat k. , v. n. To consult together; hold a consultation. maslahat mālūm h . To deem expedient. maslahat nahīṅ , Not advisable. maslahat-i-vaqt , adj. Seasonable; suited to the time. muqtazāë maslahat , G. G. Expedient.

(inf. of صلح) s. f. 1. The what is right, fit or correct, convenience, expedience, an expedient, fitness. 2. Counsel, advice. 3. Affair. مصلحت کرني maṣlaḥat karnī, To consult, to advise, to counsel, to conspire, to combine. مصلحتا maṣlaḥatā or مصلحتاً maṣlaḥataṉ, adv. Advisedly, expediently.
subs: adj. Doing that which is for good; — expert or skilled in affairs; — fitted to advise
(-se), To consult (with), to deliberate (with); to hold a consultation; to counsel, advise; — to conspire, combine
(-ko), To appear good and right (to); to be deemed advisable, or expedient
It is not right and proper; it is not advisable, or expedient
adj. Good and proper for the time or occasion; suited to the time, seasonable; opportune

ety: for A. مصلحةً, acc. of maṣlaḥat
def: adv. For the good (of); as that which is best; advisedly, expediently; opportunely.

ety: n. of place fr. سلك 'to travel; to go along (in
def: s.m. A place of passage; a passage; a way, road, path, track; — an institution; a regulation; rule (of conduct).
more: (noun of place of سلک) s. m. A way, path, track, an institution, regulation, rule of conduct.

ety: n. of place fr. سلخ 'to skin, flay'
def: s.f. 'A place in which animals are slaughtered and skinned'; slaughterhouse, shambles (syn. kamelā).
more: (noun of place of سلخ) s. f. A slaughterhouse, shambles.

def: s. (dakh.) See مسند masnad.

ety: pass. part. of سلب 'to seize, to carry off by force,' &c.
def: part. Seized, carried off by force, snatched away; spoiled, despoiled, plundered, robbed (of); stripped (of), deprived (of)
more: (part. pass. of سلب) adj. 1. Seized, snatched away. 2. Spoiled, stript, deprived of.
subs: Robbed of the senses; having the senses weakened by age, &c.; — maslūbuʼl-ḥawāsī, s.f. Unsoundness of mind, mental imbecility
adj. Bereft of reason, insane, mad.

ety: pass. part. of صلب 'to crucify'
def: part. Crucified; suspended, gibbeted.
more: part. pass. (of صلب Ch. and Syr. ) Crucified.

def: n. m. The senses weakened with age, etc. maslūb-ul-havāsī , n. f. Mental imbecility; unsoundness of mind. maslūb-ul-aql , adj. Insane; mad.

ety: pass. part. of سلك 'to travel; to go along in,' &c.
def: adj. Walked, beaten, trodden (path).
more: (part. pass. of سلک) adj. Walked on, trodden (a way).

ety: pass. part. of سلّ 'to be affected with the disease termed sill or "consumption"'
def: adj. Consumptive.

ety: masmas˚, or musmus˚, by redupl. of S. मश or मृष्+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि caus. augment
def: v.n. To breathe hard or heavily (as from emotion, or passion, &c.); to cry suppressedly or softly; to sob, snivel, whimper, &c.; to mutter, murmur, grumble; — to be highly incensed or exasperated; — to suppress (one's) sentiments from fear.
more: v. n. To suppress oneʼs sentiments from fear.

ety: pass. part. of سمع 'to hear'
def: part. adj. Heard, listened to; — audible.
more: (part. pass. of سمع Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Heard, audible.

ety: A. of مسموعة masmūʻat, fem. of masmuʻ
def: part. adj. Heard, listened to; — audible.

ety: pass. part. of سمّ 'to put poison into'
def: part. adj. Poisoned, envenomed; — venomous.
more: (part. pass. of سمّ Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Poisoned.

ety: n. of place fr. سند 'to lean, or rest' (against
def: s.f. 'A place in or upon which one leans or rests'; a couch; a throne; a large cushion (i.q. ćār-bālish); a seat, a chair; — a prop, support
more: n. m. سند 1. A cushion; seat. 2. A throne. masnad kā gadhā , n. m. 1. A puppet king. 2. A rich fool. masnad-nashīnī , n. f. Accession to the throne.
(noun of place of سند) s. f. A throne, a large cushion, a prop, a chair. مسند نشين masnad-nishīn, Sitting on a throne; a reigning prince.
subs: Adj. Adorning the throne; — s.m. A king
An enthroned jack-ass; a puppet-king; — a rich fool
adj. Sitting on the throne; — s.m. Reigning prince

ety: rel. n. fr. masnad
def: adj. Of or belonging to the masnad or throne.
more: adj. Of or belonging to the masnad or throne.

ety: rel. n. fr. A. mas̤nāt, v.n. fr. ثني 'to double'; or perhaps fr. the Hebrew Mishna
def: s.f. Poetry consisting of distichs corresponding in measure, each consisting of a pair of rhymes (besides which the distichs have distinct poetical endings); heroic verse; — a poem written in that verse (and, par excellence the poem of Jalāluʼd-dīn Moḥammaduʼl-balḵẖī, commonly called Maulavī Rūmī, who composed about A.H. 600, and whose work is regarded by his followers as of equal authority with the Qorʼān).
more: (from ثني) s. f. The sort of verse, in which the couplets rhime regularly, as in English heroick verse; a poem in that verse. (Plur. مثنويات mas̤nawīyāt ).

ety: pass. part. of صنع 'to make; to fabricate,' &c.
def: part. Formed, created, made, prepared; wrought, manufactured, constructed; invented; artificial; fictitious; fabricated, coined, forged, false, counterfeit; — s.m. A work of art or skill; an invention; — a fabrication; a contrivance.
more: (part. pass. of صنع) adj. 1. Formed, created, made, prepared. 2. Artificial. Plur. مصنوعات maṣnūʼāﻌt, s. m. Things created or artificially made, works of art, fabricks, manufactures.

ety: for A. مصنوعة maṣnūʻat, fem. of maṣnūʻ
def: part. = maṣnūʻ, q.v. part. Formed, created, made, prepared; wrought, manufactured, constructed; invented; artificial; fictitious; fabricated, coined, forged, false, counterfeit; — s.m. A work of art or skill; an invention; — a fabrication; a contrivance.
s.m. pl. (of maṣnūʻ), Created things; things artificially made; creations; works of art or skill; fabrics, manufactures; inventions; contrivances, &c.

ety: fr. muṣnūʻ
def: adj. Artificial; fictitious, &c. ( = maṣnūʻ, q.v.). part. Formed, created, made, prepared; wrought, manufactured, constructed; invented; artificial; fictitious; fabricated, coined, forged, false, counterfeit; — s.m. A work of art or skill; an invention; — a fabrication; a contrivance.

ety: pass. part. of سنّ 'to sharpen; to form, fashion; to institute,' &c.
def: part. Sharpened, whetted; polished; made smooth, or clear; — circumcised; — instituted, prescribed; rendered legal, legalized, legal, lawful, adopted into use.
more: (part. pass. of سنن) adj. 1. Legal, legalized, adopted into use, meritorious. 2. Circumcised.

def: s. m. (dakh.) Gums (of the teeth: Harris ).

ety: perf. part. & v.n. of masosnā, q.v.
def: part. adj. (f -ī), Twisted; pressed, squeezed; wrung; suppressed; — grieved, afflicted; — s.m. Pressing, pressure, squeeze; — suppressed feeling; a wrong borne in silence; — affliction, remorse, regret
more: n. m. Suppressed feelings. 1. Man kī marī kā se kahūṅ? peṭ masosā de de rahūṅ! Prov. The heart's affliction unto whom impart? silent I lay, my hands pressed 'gainst my heart! ('Who can soothe the heart's affliction,' etc.). 2. Sau kosā aur ek masosā barābar hai . Prov. One wrong in silence borne 's a sword to slay as any hundred curses you may bray.
1. adj. Twisted. 2. Grieved, afflicted. 3. s. m. Affliction, remorse, regret.
subs: To keep on pressing and bearing patiently.

ety: masos˚, i.q. masmas˚, as in masmasānā, or = S. rt. मृश् redupl.
def: v.t. To twist; to wring; to press, squeeze; to press (the heart, &c.) in order to stop its throbs of pain or anguish; to keep down, to suppress (an emotion, &c.); to bear (a wrong) patiently or in silence; to grieve, to regret: — masos karnā = masosnā.
more: v. a. 1. To twist; wring. 2. To press; squeeze. 3. To keep down or suppress (an emotion, etc).
v. a. 1. To twist. 2. To squeeze, wring. 3. To regret, to grieve.

ety: inf. n., and n. of place, fr. صرع 'to throw down'
def: s.m. Throwing down, prostrating; throw, fall; — a place where one is thrown down; — an arena (for wrestlers).

ety: for maṣrif, n. of place fr. صرف 'to expend,' &c.
def: s.m. Expenditure, expense, disbursement; cost, charge; — use.
more: n. m. Use. Kis masraf kī hai?

ety: prob. S. माष+रंग+इका
def: s.f. A kind of pulse fried.
more: s. f. A kind of pulse fried.

ety: pass. part. of صرع 'to throw down; — to suffer from epilepsy'
def: part. Thrown down, prostrated; — seized (or afflicted) with epilepsy, epileptic.
more: (part. pass. of صرع) adj. Afflicted with the epilepsy, epileptick.

ety: pass. part. of صرف 'to turn, turn back; to decline; to expend,' &c.
def: part. Turned; changed; — declined, inflected (a noun); conjugated (a verb); — expended; used; — employed, engaged (in), occupied, busy
more: adj. صرف Employed ( mashgūl ). masrūf h. , v. n. To be engaged, busy.
(part. pass. of صرف) adj. 1. Expended, employed, engaged, occupied, busy. 2. Turned, changed. 3. Declinable.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مَصْرُوف (maṣrūf).

ety: pass. part. of سرق 'to steal'
def: part. adj. Stolen, robbed; surreptitious
more: (part. pass. of سرق To steal, analog. to Chald. To empty) adj. Surreptitious, stolen, robbed.

ety: for A. مسروقة masrūqat, fem. of masrūq
def: part. adj. Stolen, robbed; surreptitious
more: adj. سرق Stolen; robbed. māl-i-masrūqah , n. m. Stolen property ( chorī kā māl ).

ety: pass. part. of سرّ 'to make glad or happy'
def: adj. Happy, joyful, glad, delighted, pleased, cheerful, exulting, gay, merry.
more: (part. pass. of سرّ) adj. Glad, cheerful, exulting, gay, delighted, pleased.

ety: fr. masrūr
def: s.f. Joy, gladness, gaiety, &c. (see masarrat).

ety: inf. n. of مسّ 'to touch'
def: s.m. Touching, feeling; touch; — sense of touch; — inclination
more: n. m. 1. Touch; sense of touch. 2. Inclination ( lagāo , 2). mas k. , v. n. To touch; feel.
(Heb. and Chald. ) s. m. Touch. مس کرنا mass karnā, To touch, to feel.

def: s.m. = مسا massā, q.v.

ety: intens. n. fr. مسح 'to measure' (land
def: s.m. Land-measurer, surveyor; — geometrician.

ety: fr. massāḥ, q.v.
def: s.f. Land-measuring, surveying.
v. a. To touch.

ety: = S. मत्त
def: adj. Intoxicated, drunk; excited, amorous, lustful, lascivious, wanton; in rut, ruttish; rampant; furious, mad, insane; proud (of, -meṅ), arrogant; — elated, transported, overjoyed, delighted; — s.m. A drunkard; — a proud person; — a madman
more: adj. See madh (1, 3, 6). 1. Drink; intoxicated. 2. Excited; hot; lascivious. Mastāī bakrī bok kā mūṅh chūmtī hai . Prov. The she-goat heated licks the buck-goat's mouth. 3. Overjoyed; transported; beside oneself. 4. Rampant. 5. Careless; indifferent. 6. Mad. Rizālā mast huā, Khudā ko bhūl gayā . Prov. mast, mastānah , n. m. 1. A drunkard, 2. A madman. mast-bok , n. m. H. bok = Eng. buck, from the sound the animal utters. A lusty goat. mast mahīnā , n. m. The lusty month of Feb.— March when the sexual passions are most excited. Mast mahīnā phāgan kā. mast nāth, yā maulā , n. m. A drunken, careless person. bad-mast , Dead-drunk. māl-mast , n. m. One who is purse-proud.
adj. Drunk, intoxicated, lustful, lascivious, wanton, proud. (Pers. plur. مستان).
subs: s.m. 'The month of lust, or excitement, or passion,' the month of February
or mast-maulā, s.m. A drunken, careless fellow
adj. & s.m. Purse-proud; — one who is purse-proud.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مست (mast).

ety: for A. مصطبة maṣt̤abat, &c.
def: s.m. A place where people assemble; — a hospice (for strangers), an inn, caravanserai.
more: s. m. An inn or place of reception for strangers.

def: s.m. The head, skull; the forehead; top or highest part (of anything), summit (of a mountain).
more: n. m. The forehead, esp. of an elephant.
s. m. f. 1. The head. 2. The forehead.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit मस्तक (mastaka).
